source_files: Elicit - Gut Microbiome and Health A Bidirectional Review - Report.pdf

findings.description: Mediterranean dietary pattern characterized by high intake of plant-based foods, healthy fats, and moderate fish consumption
Diet rich in dietary fiber from whole grains, legumes, fruits, and vegetables
Dietary pattern characterized by high intake of processed foods, refined carbohydrates, and animal fat products
cautions: Associated with dysbiosis and pro-inflammatory microbiota composition
Reduced beneficial taxa and SCFA production

findings.description: Diet primarily composed of plant-derived foods including fruits, vegetables, legumes, and whole grains; may include dairy and eggs but excludes or minimizes animal flesh
Diet composed primarily of animal-derived foods including meat, poultry, fish, and dairy products with minimal plant-based foods
cautions: Potential negative effects on barrier function

findings.description: Very low-carbohydrate, high-fat dietary pattern typically containing <5% carbohydrate and >70% fat
clinical_outcomes: Reduced beneficial taxa despite improved metrics
cautions: Improved diversity metrics but reduced beneficial taxa
Decreased Bifidobacterium abundance may impact immune function

findings.description: Dietary approach restricting fermentable oligosaccharides, disaccharides, monosaccharides, and polyols to reduce symptom burden in IBS
mechanisms: Symptom reduction via decreased gas production
cautions: Symptom improvement but microbial depletion risk
Reduced SCFA-producing bacteria despite symptom relief
Long-term restriction may compromise microbiota diversity and function

findings.description: Dietary intervention with reduced total energy intake, typically 20-40% below baseline requirements
Effects of specific macronutrient quality and quantity on microbial composition independent of dietary pattern

dietary_fiber.description: Soluble and insoluble fiber from whole grains, legumes, fruits, vegetables

protein_quality.description: Amino acid composition and source (animal vs plant protein)

fat_composition.description: Saturated vs unsaturated fatty acid intake
mechanisms: Unsaturated fatty acids affected microbial richness
Dairy consumption correlated with Lactobacillaceae abundance

whole_grains.description: Intact cereal grains with bran and germ
mechanisms: Associated inversely with microbial dysbiosis scores
Salt intake positively correlated with Lactobacillaceae

findings.description: Foods naturally enriched in polyphenolic compounds including red wine, tea, berries, nuts, and plant-derived foods
mechanisms: Enhanced metabolic production of bioactive metabolites

red_wine.description: Polyphenol-rich wine containing resveratrol and other compounds

tea.description: Brewed leaves of Camellia sinensis containing catechins and theaflavins

clinical_outcomes: Reduced opportunistic pathogens including Streptococcus agalactiae
cautions: Polyphenol effects dependent on gut microbiota composition and capacity to metabolize them
Host individual differences in enterotype and metabolic capacity

findings.description: Non-caloric or low-caloric sugar substitutes including aspartame, saccharin, sucralose, and others
mechanisms: Associated with significant differences in circulating lipid acid metabolites
clinical_outcomes: Variable metabolic effects depending on microbiota composition
cautions: Microbiome composition mediates dietary effects of artificial sweeteners

findings.description: Time-dependent effects of dietary interventions on microbial composition and functional capacity
mechanisms: Adult microbiota exhibits remarkable compositional stability with transient fluctuations
Microbiota rapidly returns to baseline composition after short-term dietary challenges
Metabolic profiles change substantially within 24 hours despite modest taxonomic alterations
Functional flexibility allows different metabolic pathways activation independent of compositional shifts
clinical_significance: Explains why metaeomic signatures may respond more rapidly than taxonomic profiles
Suggests functional redundancy in microbiota metabolic capacity
Indicates potential for rapid activation of different metabolic pathways with dietary modification
cautions: Short-term microbiota shifts may not predict long-term compositional changes
Individual microbiota baseline composition affects dietary response trajectories

aslam_2026.authors: Hajara Aslam, G. Trakman, Thusharika Dissanayake, et al.
aslam_2026.title: Dietary interventions and the gut microbiota: a systematic literature review of 80 controlled clinical trials

qureshi_2025.authors: Umer Qureshi, Ali Bajwa, Zohaib Aslam, et al.
qureshi_2025.title: Gut Microbiota Modulation in Type 2 Diabetes and Cardiometabolic Risk: A Systematic Review

bolte_2021.authors: L. Bolte, A. Vich Vila, F. Imhann, et al.
bolte_2021.title: Long-term dietary patterns are associated with pro-inflammatory and anti-inflammatory features of the gut microbiome

czarnota_2025.authors: Maksymilian Czarnota, Weronika Ossowska, Wiktoria Osztreiher
czarnota_2025.title: Gut Microbiota Modulation to Enhance Exercise Performance and Recovery - Systematic Review

zeng_2025.authors: Qin Zeng, Xianqiong Feng, Yanling Hu, Shaoyu Su
zeng_2025.title: The human gut microbiota is associated with host lifestyle: a comprehensive narrative review

bautista_2025.authors: Jhommara Bautista, Sofia Ojeda-Mosquera, Adriana Altamirano-Colina, et al.
bautista_2025.title: Bidirectional interactions between circadian rhythms and the gut microbiome

wang_2025.authors: Shun Wang, Zihan Bao, Mengqi Zhao, et al.
wang_2025.title: Impact of Dietary Pattern Interventions on Gut Microbiota in Obesity: a Systematic Review and Meta-analysis

lee_2026.authors: T. Lee, S. Fehily, G. Trakman, et al.
lee_2026.title: The Impact of dietary intervention on the gastrointestinal microbiome in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: a systematic review

tang_2019.authors: Zheng-Zheng Tang, Guanhua Chen, Q. Hong, et al.
tang_2019.title: Multi-Omic Analysis of the Microbiome and Metabolome in Healthy Subjects Reveals Microbiome-Dependent Relationships Between Diet and Metabolites

table_mapping.description: Dietary Pattern → Microbial Changes → Clinical Outcomes (extracted from Table on page 19)

rows.clinical_outcomes: Reduced inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-1β, IL-6) [1]; improved metabolic health [2]

rows.microbial_changes: Increased Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus, Lachnospira [1]; elevated butyrate producers [2]
rows.clinical_outcomes: Lowered fasting glucose and HbA1c [2]; improved lipid profiles [2]

rows.microbial_changes: Reduced SCFA producers (Butyricicoccus, Lachnospiraceae) [1]; increased pathogens (Bilophila, Gemella) [1]
Increased butyrate and anti-inflammatory bacteria [1]; elevated Prevotella [47]
rows.clinical_outcomes: Improved metabolic profiles [47]; enhanced gut barrier function [3]

rows.microbial_changes: Reduced butyrate producers (Ruminococcus bromii, Eubacterium rectale) [1]
rows.clinical_outcomes: Increased inflammation [3]; altered bile acid metabolism [50]

rows.microbial_changes: Increased Akkermansia and F/B ratio [37]; reduced Bifidobacterium [37]
rows.clinical_outcomes: Improved diversity metrics but reduced beneficial taxa [37]
Symptom improvement in IBS [119] but microbial depletion [38]

notes.fiber_threshold: ≥14g per 1000kcal correlates positively with Coprococcus, Lachnospira, and Roseburia genera [45]
notes.temporal_dynamics: Adult gut microbiota exhibits remarkable compositional stability with transient fluctuations from short-term dietary changes [135]. Metabolic profiles change substantially within 24 hours despite modest compositional shifts, suggesting functional flexibility and rapid activation of different metabolic pathways independent of compositional changes [135]
notes.polyphenol_effects: Polyphenol-rich foods demonstrate selective bidogenic effects and promoted beneficial taxa. Red wine and tea polyphenols increased Bifidobacterium and Lactobacillus populations [72], while effects are dependent on gut enterotype [50]

cautions_summary.low_fodmap: Symptom improvement in IBS but risks microbial depletion and reduced SCFA-producing bacteria
cautions_summary.ketogenic: Improved diversity metrics but reduced beneficial taxa (Bifidobacterium); long-term sustainability concerns
cautions_summary.western_diet: Associated with dysbiosis, reduced beneficial taxa, increased pathogenic bacteria, and elevated inflammation
cautions_summary.artificial_sweeteners: Effects dependent on host enterotype; significant inter-individual heterogeneity in response
